ref: Jesus asked them, “What does the Law of Moses say about this?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ba se̱ŋbise bo̱ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa mo̱ ne̱ Mosis a sa mo̱ne̱?”
translated: And Jesus, when the people were worshiping and praying, said, "Why did you tell Moses about me?"

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Ɔ maa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ a o gywii ase̱sɛ na a mo̱ŋ gye̱ kitee bɛɛɛ ke̱kpare̱. Amaa amo̱ ya saŋ mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱, ne̱ ase̱sɛ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, amɔ ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ itee na akpare̱‑ɔ kaase̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ afwii na alaŋkpare̱ a bu Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo
translated: He will teach service, and you perform services, and that you do not quickly know the little truth. Also go there and do not say that you are not worthy of their great praise, and the services are not in good condition, and he takes much but does not give food, and the small Lord who is called Jesus (Matthew)

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ko̱tɔko̱‑ɔ, fo̱ i de, sa mò̱ ke̱tɔ‑ɔ. Ɔko̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ para ko̱tɔko̱, bo̱ para mò̱.
translated: The boy looks at his house, seeing that it is there, but he does not enter his house. He says that he builds a house, but he does not build.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ na mò̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ ne̱ baa dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a yo̱rɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ŋu atɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i ŋu ndɔɔ a kake mɔ‑ɔ, na bo̱ nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i nu mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ. Amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ sa bamo̱ kpa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ kitee a kaase̱‑ɔ (Maak
translated: I tell the story that people have heard about Wuribware: that we go, that they come to see the chief’s house, concerning your speaking with your mouth—that you shut your mouth to stop it—and your hand working—that you not do evil with your hand. Indeed I speak these words. And that Jesus was hung on the cross and suffered (Mark).

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kra mò̱ kayaagyi amo̱, ne̱ baa gyiiri mò̱ le̱e̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa mɔɔ mò̱.
translated: Amo̱se̱ and they don’t say good things to me, and they don’t listen to their words, and they don’t come.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a wu. Mò̱ a wu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ Yo̱sɛf se̱ dee‑ro Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.
translated: Prince Herod said. But he said that in the worship house he commanded Joseph to kill the people of Egypt.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Yooo, mo̱ a nu. Mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ kya mò̱.”
translated: Now Yessu says, “Hey, I don’t know. They say take this thing to me.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a baa agyabɔɔ na ate̱napo̱ na asare̱e̱ wusɛ awuye na mmu na bamo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱‑ɔ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱.
translated: Mfeŋ and the community are afraid because they cannot act. The youth group goes fighting, stealing, and killing with weapons, and the people and the youth take guns and continuously do not stop. And this is bad for the youth.

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waare̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱fɔ mò̱ kuri mò̱ nyare̱‑ana e̱ kye̱na ako̱ŋ na ɔ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi.
translated: Jesus was standing among the people saying, “Listen, what I am saying is true: the work that they see—do the works that they do not know nor see, do they perform them by their own power, indeed? I truly teach.”

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Bo̱ fo̱ kanye‑o, Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ a ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a fo̱ nkyu nse̱na, na Yeesuu mɔ wo̱re̱ bo̱ bo̱ŋbe̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱‑ɔ se̱.
translated: Now the people say, Jesus has indeed come, the word he speaks is good news, and Jesus saves our lives and lives with us forever.

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: To, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba gywii abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ de bodobodoo‑o si ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ?”
translated: Now, the gathering people and the chiefs and rulers of the community say, "I/We, my/our people are in trouble because of those my/our chiefs—is that not so?"

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii soogyaa amo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nare̱ pe̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o e̱ waa a ke̱ sa fo̱.” Saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱‑rɔ dɛɛ ne̱ soogyaa amo̱ a ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ a kpaare̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ (Maak
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, saying: “Hear O, look at this thing, you will truly understand what you do.” Then all his words were heard by his disciples, who listened carefully, and he concluded. Moreover, Jesus performed many miracles (Maak

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ne̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ a kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱.
translated: Jesus said he gave the word of commandments

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Farisii awuye ko̱ a ŋu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, fo̱ agyase̱po̱ a ke̱gyɛɛ abwaye fee‑o gye̱ kusuŋ. Ne̱ a kye ane̱ mbraa fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a waa kusuŋ ko̱ kukyure kake.”
translated: The Pharisees came and spoke to him, and they questioned Jesus, saying, “Hear, you hypocrites! You are making your teaching words empty. And you judge by what you say—the same words that you twist.”

ref: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
text: Ne̱ kake ŋko̱ ɔɔ ya ŋu ɔko̱ de ane̱ŋ a ataŋgyi‑o ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ ko̱. Amaa ke̱mo̱ yawo̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ fɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ oo kperi ke̱mo̱ se̱ yɔ pe̱ ya fe mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ ya sɔɔ ke̱taŋgyi amo̱.” Kisawu kitee
translated: The people who live under this tree farm together as it is said. But their work is not really easy. Those who understand this work say that they still want to keep planting without finishing. Indeed so.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a wu. Mò̱ a wu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ Yo̱sɛf se̱ dee‑ro Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.
translated: Prince Herod is here. But he says that the elders' house and they went to Joseph and told him about the land of Egypt.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.

translated: Then Yeesuu says, “Hey, I am not. You tell me not to let me know.”

text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bamo̱ bo̱ko̱ a baa agyabɔɔ na ate̱napo̱ na asare̱e̱ wusɛ awuye na mmu na bamo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi e̱wo̱re̱se̱ e̱wo̱re̱se̱‑ɔ mò̱ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱.
translated: The people work continuously but do not work properly. They even go hunting with empty hands and hunger, with sickness and death, yet they still go to the market completely empty and get nothing. But this affects them.

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waare̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱fɔ mò̱ kuri mò̱ nyare̱‑ana e̱ kye̱na ako̱ŋ na ɔ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi.
translated: Jesus stood among the people and said, “The things I have told you are the truth that dwells in my house. Shall my house not perish and not be abandoned, and will you guard it until I return, indeed? Truly I pray.”
